_comment: COG-014: Task-specific lessons for perception domain A/B fixtures. Scope 'perception' is matched by the reflection_db scope filter when the fixture entity list contains a perception-domain term. These directives emphasise entity extraction, risk-signal handling, and ambiguity resolution — the core hypotheses tested by perception_tasks.json.

directives.directive: When the prompt contains a file path, quoted identifier, or dotted module name, treat it as a named entity that must be verified before acting — do not invent its contents.
When risk markers are present (rm, delete, force, drop, truncate, urgent), surface the exact command and request explicit confirmation before executing any destructive action.
When the prompt is ambiguous (vague reference, pronoun with no antecedent, 'it', 'that thing'), ask a focused clarifying question instead of guessing intent.
When multiple entities are detected in a single prompt, resolve each one independently — do not assume they all refer to the same object.
Constraints stated with MUST, always, or never are hard requirements — extract them explicitly and honour them throughout the response.
